title: mentha austriaca var. genuina
intro: Mentha austriaca var. genuina, also known as Austrian mint, is a subspecies of mint native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its distinctive square stems and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of menthol and other essential oils. The health benefits of Austrian mint include relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, as well as its potential to ease respiratory problems, such as congestion and coughs. Austrian mint can be prepared as a tea, added to topical creams or ointments, or used in cooking and herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
